     Sources: src/demos/, src/content/components/, src/data/features.ts -->

# Tooltip

Two tiers — a pure :hover/:focus tooltip that works everywhere, and interest-invoker toolbar tips with smart delays and viewport-aware flipping.

There are two ways to build a tooltip here. The **plain-CSS** one works in every browser today and is the right default for a lone
control. The **interest-invoker** one is cutting-edge (Chromium only, for now) and earns its
keep the moment you have a *group* of controls, because it can dynamically adjust the display delay.

## Plain CSS - maximum compatibility

```html
<span class="group/tip relative inline-flex">
  <button
    aria-describedby="tip-library"
    class="inline-flex h-9 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center gap-2 rounded-lg border border-input bg-transparent px-4 text-sm font-medium whitespace-nowrap text-foreground transition-colors hover:bg-accent focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring"
  >
    Hover or focus me
  </button>
  <span
    id="tip-library"
    role="tooltip"
    class="pointer-events-none absolute bottom-[calc(100%+8px)] left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 translate-y-1 rounded-md bg-primary px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs whitespace-nowrap text-primary-foreground opacity-0 transition-[opacity,translate] delay-100 group-hover/tip:translate-y-0 group-hover/tip:opacity-100 group-focus-within/tip:translate-y-0 group-focus-within/tip:opacity-100"
  >
    Add to library
  </span>
</span>
```

The safe default. No new platform features, no build caveats, works everywhere.

- A `group/tip` wrapper toggles the tip via `group-hover/tip:` and `group-focus-within/tip:`,
  so keyboard users get it too.
- `delay-100` waits a beat before showing, so a cursor passing through doesn't flash it.
- `pointer-events-none` keeps the tip from stealing hover and flickering itself.

The trade-off: because it's positioned by hand, it can't flip at a viewport edge, so keep tips
short. And, as the next section explains, a *row* of these can't get the toolbar delay behavior
right. For one button in the middle of a form, this is all you need.

## Interest invokers - cutting edge, built for groups

```html
<div class="toolbar-tips flex items-center rounded-lg border border-border bg-card divide-x divide-border [&>button:first-of-type]:rounded-l-[7px] [&>button]:[interest-delay-start:0.3s] [&:has(:interest-source)>button]:[interest-delay-start:0s] [&>button:last-of-type]:rounded-r-[7px] [&>span]:border-0">
  <button
    type="button"
    interestfor="tt-bold"
    aria-label="Bold"
    class="inline-flex size-8 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center transition-colors hover:bg-accent focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring [anchor-name:--tt-bold]"
  >
    <svg class="size-4" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true">
      <path d="M6 12h9a4 4 0 0 1 0 8H7a1 1 0 0 1-1-1V5a1 1 0 0 1 1-1h7a4 4 0 0 1 0 8"></path>
    </svg>
  </button>
  <span
    id="tt-bold"
    popover="hint"
    class="pointer-events-none my-1.5 rounded-md bg-primary px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs whitespace-nowrap text-primary-foreground translate-y-1 opacity-0 transition-[opacity,translate,overlay,display] transition-discrete duration-100 open:translate-y-0 open:opacity-100 starting:open:translate-y-1 starting:open:opacity-0 [position-anchor:--tt-bold] [position-area:top] [position-try-fallbacks:flip-block]"
    >Bold</span
  >

  <button
    type="button"
    interestfor="tt-italic"
    aria-label="Italic"
    class="inline-flex size-8 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center transition-colors hover:bg-accent focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring [anchor-name:--tt-italic]"
  >
    <svg class="size-4" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true">
      <line x1="19" x2="10" y1="4" y2="4"></line>
      <line x1="14" x2="5" y1="20" y2="20"></line>
      <line x1="15" x2="9" y1="4" y2="20"></line>
    </svg>
  </button>
  <span
    id="tt-italic"
    popover="hint"
    class="pointer-events-none my-1.5 rounded-md bg-primary px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs whitespace-nowrap text-primary-foreground translate-y-1 opacity-0 transition-[opacity,translate,overlay,display] transition-discrete duration-100 open:translate-y-0 open:opacity-100 starting:open:translate-y-1 starting:open:opacity-0 [position-anchor:--tt-italic] [position-area:top] [position-try-fallbacks:flip-block]"
    >Italic</span
  >

  <button
    type="button"
    interestfor="tt-underline"
    aria-label="Underline"
    class="inline-flex size-8 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center transition-colors hover:bg-accent focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring [anchor-name:--tt-underline]"
  >
    <svg class="size-4" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true">
      <path d="M6 4v6a6 6 0 0 0 12 0V4"></path>
      <line x1="4" x2="20" y1="20" y2="20"></line>
    </svg>
  </button>
  <span
    id="tt-underline"
    popover="hint"
    class="pointer-events-none my-1.5 rounded-md bg-primary px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs whitespace-nowrap text-primary-foreground translate-y-1 opacity-0 transition-[opacity,translate,overlay,display] transition-discrete duration-100 open:translate-y-0 open:opacity-100 starting:open:translate-y-1 starting:open:opacity-0 [position-anchor:--tt-underline] [position-area:top] [position-try-fallbacks:flip-block]"
    >Underline</span
  >

  <button
    type="button"
    interestfor="tt-link"
    aria-label="Insert link"
    class="inline-flex size-8 cursor-pointer items-center justify-center transition-colors hover:bg-accent focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-ring [anchor-name:--tt-link] border-0"
  >
    <svg class="size-4" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" aria-hidden="true">
      <path d="M10 13a5 5 0 0 0 7.54.54l3-3a5 5 0 0 0-7.07-7.07l-1.72 1.71"></path>
      <path d="M14 11a5 5 0 0 0-7.54-.54l-3 3a5 5 0 0 0 7.07 7.07l1.71-1.71"></path>
    </svg>
  </button>
  <span
    id="tt-link"
    popover="hint"
    class="pointer-events-none my-1.5 rounded-md bg-primary px-2.5 py-1.5 text-xs whitespace-nowrap text-primary-foreground translate-y-1 opacity-0 transition-[opacity,translate,overlay,display] transition-discrete duration-100 open:translate-y-0 open:opacity-100 starting:open:translate-y-1 starting:open:opacity-0 [position-anchor:--tt-link] [position-area:top] [position-try-fallbacks:flip-block]"
    >Insert link</span
  >
</div>
```

The editor-toolbar pattern: the **first** tooltip waits 300ms, but once one is showing, moving
to the next button switches instantly. The delay resets the moment you move the mouse away.

- `interestfor` on each button points at its tip, a `popover="hint"` element. The browser
  handles showing on hover **and** keyboard focus, hiding, delays, and the top layer. No
  `group`/`peer` wiring, no `pointer-events` guard.
- The delay trick (via Emil Kowalski), written once on the wrapper:
  `[&>button]:[interest-delay-start:0.3s]` makes the first tooltip wait, and
  `[&:has(:interest-source)>button]:[interest-delay-start:0s]` zeroes the delay while *any*
  sibling already shows interest. Moving between buttons switches tips instantly. The default
  `interest-delay-end` keeps a button's interest "warm" for a moment after you leave it. That
  overlap is what makes the switch feel instant, so don't set it to `0s`.
- Every button declares its own `[anchor-name:--tt-*]` and its tip's `[position-anchor:--tt-*]`
  points back at it, so `[position-area:top]` centers each tip over its own button.
- `[position-try-fallbacks:flip-block]` keeps tips on screen: when a tip would poke past the
  top of the viewport, the browser flips it below the button automatically.

## The fallback story

In browsers without interest invokers the toolbar's hover tips never appear. The buttons
keep their `aria-label`s, so assistive tech loses nothing. If you need hover tooltips for
everyone today, use the plain-CSS variant. A *row* of them will then share one fixed
delay rather than the adaptive toolbar timing.

## Leading-edge features used (and what happens without them)

- **Interest invokers (interestfor + popover="hint")** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Global_attributes/interestfor))
  Without it: The hover UI simply never appears — triggers keep their aria-labels and links still navigate, so nothing is lost. If you need it everywhere today, the plain-CSS technique on the Tooltip page works in every browser.
- **CSS anchor positioning** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_anchor_positioning))
  Without it: The popover opens centered in the viewport (the top-layer default) instead of attached to its trigger.
- **Entry/exit animations (allow-discrete + @starting-style)** ([MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/transition-behavior))
  Without it: Elements appear and disappear instantly. Nothing breaks — you just lose the animation.
